name: Pipecat (Voice Agent Framework)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD 2-Clause); integrates with paid services (Deepgram, ElevenLabs, etc.)

auth.notes: API keys for integrated services (STT, LLM, TTS providers)

performance.notes: Pipeline latency depends on STT+LLM+TTS; optimized for <1s end-to-end with fast providers

agent_readiness.notes: Open-source framework for building real-time voice AI agents. Pipeline architecture: audio input → STT → LLM → TTS → audio output. WebRTC transport (Daily.co, LiveKit). Multiple STT integrations (Deepgram, OpenAI, WhisperLive). Multiple LLM integrations (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini). Multiple TTS (ElevenLabs, Cartesia, OpenAI). Interruption handling. Function calling integration. Used to build phone AI agents, voice assistants, customer service bots.

gotchas: Rapidly evolving API — breaking changes expected
Requires managing multiple API keys (STT + LLM + TTS)
Latency highly dependent on provider selection
Newer project — less battle-tested than Twilio-based solutions
best_when: Building voice AI agents and phone bots
avoid_when: When commercial voice platforms (VAPI, Bland.ai) are acceptable
summary: Pipecat is an open-source framework for building real-time voice AI agents. Pipeline architecture: audio → STT → LLM → TTS → audio. Supports Daily.co/LiveKit WebRTC, multiple STT/LLM/TTS providers, interruption handling, and function calling. Best for building phone AI agents and voice assistants. Rapidly evolving — expect API changes.
